name: RapidFuzz (Fast Fuzzy String Matching)

performance.notes: C++ Cython extension; 10-100x faster than fuzzywuzzy; SIMD optimizations

agent_readiness.notes: Fast fuzzy string matching. from rapidfuzz import fuzz, process. fuzz.ratio('hello', 'helo'). fuzz.partial_ratio('hello world', 'hello'). fuzz.token_sort_ratio('hello world', 'world hello'). fuzz.token_set_ratio(). process.extractOne('query', choices). process.extract('query', choices, limit=5). process.cdist(queries, choices, scorer=fuzz.ratio). Score cutoff: process.extract(query, choices, score_cutoff=70). Batch: rapidfuzz.process.cdist(). Drop-in for thefuzz/fuzzywuzzy with scorer= parameter.

gotchas: token_sort_ratio and token_set_ratio have different semantics for partial matches
cdist can OOM for large matrices — use chunked processing
Different defaults from thefuzz — check scorer parameter compatibility
best_when: Entity deduplication and record linkage at scale
Batch pairwise string similarity with cdist
avoid_when: Very long strings where NLP approaches work better
summary: RapidFuzz provides fast fuzzy string matching (10-100x faster than fuzzywuzzy). fuzz.ratio(), partial_ratio(), token_sort_ratio(). process.extractOne(query, choices). cdist for batch pairwise similarity. Score cutoff parameter. MIT by Max Bachmann. C++ SIMD backend. Drop-in replacement for thefuzz. Best for entity deduplication, record linkage, and fuzzy search at scale.
